title: Daily Briefing — Apr 30
headline: Flows support, but bonds wobble—eyes open for cracks, don't chase new highs.
body: Right, let's look at what the flows are telling us: SPY, QQQ, XLK all approaching new highs and above their 20 MAs, with tech leading the pack (XLK at $159.11, SMA20 $149.13). This is a bull trend, but the Fear & Greed index has cooled from extreme—now 63.4 (greed) vs last month’s 13.7 (abject fear). Volatility (VIX 18.02) is normal and contracting, so the algo crowd is back in force.

Sector rotation is clear: Energy (XLE $59.03, SMA20 $57.28) and tech are driving, with financials and healthcare improving but still flagged by elevated risk. Industrials (XLI $169.93, SMA20 $170.43) and staples remain lagging. Don't assume leadership lasts forever—cycle phase matters. XLRE just triggered above all MAs; the rotational play is real estate for mean reversion.

Contrarian lens: Bonds (TLT $85.70, below SMA20 $86.63) and HYG ($80.13, approaching SMA20 $80.22) are breaking down—R001 triggered. If bonds freak out, everything breaks, so reduce equity exposure any time credit rolls over. COIN (-6.4%) and CHTR (-8.3%) are warning shots from financials and comms; the canary in the coal mine is twitching. Abundance mindset—don’t chase, find the next setup. If it's in the press, it's in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: $711.58 — watch for a daily close < SMA20 $692.09
XLK: $159.11 — leadership zone, SMA20 $149.13
XLE: $59.03 — leading, SMA20 $57.28
TLT/HYG: Both triggered breakdowns — flag for risk
COIN: $181.73 — weak, below SMA20
XLRE: $43.64 — triggered, above all MAs
Fear & Greed cooling from extreme — not a sell, but caution
action_items: Reduce equity exposure if TLT/HYG continue breaking down on daily closes (apply R001).
Watch XLRE for rotational mean reversion and real estate momentum above all MAs; don't chase tech highs, wait for pullbacks to 20 MA.
closing_quote: Patience, react—don't predict. If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Simple as that.
